content.problemStatement:
Chef knows NNN customers will come to his bakery today, and so he has baked MMM cakes (M≥NM \ge NM≥N).

Every customer needs at least one cake, otherwise they will for sure be unhappy. Further, if a customer is able to buy **two** or more cakes, then they will be happy.

Chef wants to maximize the number of happy customers while not having any unhappy customers. What is the maximum possible happy customers?


Input Format

The first and only line of each test case contains 222 integers - NNN and MMM.


Output Format
Output the maximum number of happy customers.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Chef can make all the customers happy by letting each buy 222 cakes, and having 111 cake leftover.

Explanation:
Chef can let customers 111 and 222 buy 222 cakes, and customers 333 and 444 buy 111 cake each.
